page: Microsoft Azure GS5-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a single thread per core. A key characteristic of this instance is its massive memory capacity of 448.0 GB, yielding an exceptionally high resource density of 56.0 GB of RAM per core. It also includes 918 GB of local storage, while lacking any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is weak, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng and the bottom 25% on Geekbench. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth read/write operations fall into the average range. For example, the server achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 5342.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 11.02 million operations per second. Cryptographic and decompression workloads show weaker results, reflecting the older CPU architecture and lack of AVX-512 instructions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS5-8 represents a specialized profile where the budget is heavily allocated toward memory capacity rather than raw compute speed. This makes it a poor fit for compute-intensive or single-threaded tasks. Instead, the server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads that require large datasets to reside in RAM, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, large-scale database operations, and enterprise applications requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios.
description: Microsoft Azure GS5-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance is defined by its massive memory allocation of 448.0 GB, which equates to 56.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 918 GB of local storage. Benchmark data highlights a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. Given this hardware profile, the GS5-8 is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound applications rather than compute-heavy tasks. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring massive memory capacity relative to processing cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS5-8 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 448 GB of RAM, and 918 GB of local storage, offering high memory density for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure GS5-8 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 448 GB of RAM, and 918 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring massive RAM capacity and local storage for memory-bound enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Massive 448.0 GB of RAM providing 56.0 GB per core
Weak single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth
Designed for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
